name: Current Affairs in TN History/Culture/Heritage (3): Keeladi Open-Air Museum & New Regional Museums

sourceRefs.label: dtnext.in/indiaherald.com - Keeladi Open-Air Museum inauguration (14 Feb 2026); Chola Museum at Tamil University; Noyyal & Navai Museums

researchNotes: Third TN Culture current-affairs topic (3rd lap), completing the 3rd full rotation cycle through all 6 Part A units. Directly extends the Day-48 Keezhadi topic and the Day-62 archaeology current-affairs topic (iron-object discoveries) with the newest, most concrete development: a purpose-built museum AT the excavation site itself.

reading.intro: Covers the newly inaugurated Keeladi Open-Air Museum and related new regional museum projects in Tamil Nadu — extending the project's existing Keezhadi excavation coverage with the latest concrete development.

qa.question: When was the Keeladi Open-Air Museum inaugurated, by whom, and how?
qa.answer: 14 February 2026, by Chief Minister M.K. Stalin, via video conferencing.

qa.question: What is distinctive about this museum compared to a typical archaeology museum?
qa.answer: It is believed to be the first facility of its kind in India where archaeological remains are displayed IN their original context/surroundings — i.e., built directly at the excavation site, rather than artifacts being moved to a separate museum building elsewhere.

qa.question: What is the Keeladi Open-Air Museum's area and cost?
qa.answer: Developed across 4.5 acres at a cost of ₹24 crore, with two exhibition halls (combined built-up area of 65,380 sq. ft.), positioned about 80 metres apart.

qa.question: What structural remains (over 2,000 years old) are preserved and displayed at the site?
qa.answer: Brick walls, ring wells, terracotta pipelines, and drainage systems — from the 2nd, 5th, and 6th excavation phases at Keeladi.

qa.question: What other museum was announced (at the same event) for the Chola dynasty, and where?
qa.answer: A dedicated Chola Museum at Tamil University's Thanjavur campus, with an estimated budget of ₹56.14 crore.

qa.question: What other two regional museums had their foundation stones laid at the same event?
qa.answer: The Noyyal Museum (Erode district) and the Navai Museum (Ramanathapuram district).

questions.prompt: The Keeladi Open-Air Museum was inaugurated on:
What makes the Keeladi Open-Air Museum distinctive among Indian archaeology museums?
options: It is the largest museum in India
It displays remains in their original excavation context/site
questions.explanation: Displays remains in their original excavation context/site — first such facility in India.
